title: Huawei Band 11 Pro officially arrives in Nepal with built-in location tracking
summary: Huawei has launched the Band 11 Pro as a higher-end version of the regular Band 11 in Nepal, and it brings interesting upgrades over. Because for the first time in this lineup, you now get built-in GPS to track your location routes. The Huawei Band 11 Pro also has other interesting features to its name, so without further ado, let me tell you all about it, including its official price in Nepal, sp
content:
Huawei has launched the Band 11 Pro as a higher-end version of the regular Band 11 in Nepal, and it brings interesting upgrades over. Because for the first time in this lineup, you now get built-in GPS to track your location routes. The Huawei Band 11 Pro also has other interesting features to its name, so without further ado, let me tell you all about it, including its official price in Nepal, specifications, and availability. Advertisement Huawei Band 11 Pro Overview Design and Display At first glance, the Band 11 Pro looks very similar to the regular Band 11. The overall shape and screen remain the same, but the Pro model is slightly larger at 43.5 mm tall and a bit heavier at around 18 grams. Unlike the standard Band 11, which comes in both polymer and aluminum versions, the Pro model uses an aluminum alloy case across the board. Similarly, you are getting the Band 11 Pro in different colour options that include Green, Blue, and Black with fluoroelastomer straps. Water resistance remains the same at 5 ATM. On the other side, the display itself stays unchanged. You still get a 1.62-inch AMOLED panel with a 286 × 482 resolution and 347 PPI density, while it has 2,000 nits of peak brightness versus 1,500 nits on the regular Band 11. Connectivity and Features The biggest difference between the two is built-in GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System). The standard Band 11 relies on connected GPS from your phone, but the Pro model can track outdoor workouts on its own. Huawei also highlights upgraded health tracking features. The Band 11 Pro adds pulse wave arrhythmia analysis and updated sleep tracking tools. Also, read Health and Smart Features You still get the usual Huawei wearable features like continuous heart rate monitoring, SpO₂ tracking, sleep tracking, stress monitoring, and emotional wellbeing tools. The band uses a 9-axis IMU sensor system along with an optical heart rate sensor and an ambient light sensor. It connects to the Huawei Health app and supports Android 9 and above, and iOS 13 and above. Battery life is also unchanged from the regular Band 11. Huawei claims up to 14 days on light use, around 8 days on typical usage, and roughly 3 days with Always On Display enabled. Huawei Band 11 Pro Specifications Design, build: Aluminium case, 42.6 × 28.2 × 8.99 mm, 18 gm weight, 5 ATM water resistance Display: 1.62-inch AMOLED, 286 × 482 resolution, 347 PPI, up to 2,000 nits, 60Hz, Always On Display Software & UI: HarmonyOS 6.0, Huawei Health app integration Connectivity: Bluetooth 6.0, BLE, Built-in GNSS Sensors: 9-axis IMU, optical heart rate sensor, ambient light sensor Battery: Up to 14 days under light use Huawei Band 11 Pro Price in Nepal and Availability In Nepal, the official price of the Huawei Band 11 Pro is NPR 14,990. You can buy this fitness band in Nepal from Huawei-authorized stores across the country. VIDEO

At first glance, the Band 11 Pro looks very similar to the regular Band 11. The overall shape and screen remain the same, but the Pro model is slightly larger at 43.5 mm tall and a bit heavier at around 18 grams. Unlike the standard Band 11, which comes in both polymer and aluminum versions, the Pro model uses an aluminum alloy case across the board.

Similarly, you are getting the Band 11 Pro in different colour options that include Green, Blue, and Black with fluoroelastomer straps. Water resistance remains the same at 5 ATM. On the other side, the display itself stays unchanged. You still get a 1.62-inch AMOLED panel with a 286 × 482 resolution and 347 PPI density, while it has 2,000 nits of peak brightness versus 1,500 nits on the regular Band 11.

The biggest difference between the two is built-in GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System). The standard Band 11 relies on connected GPS from your phone, but the Pro model can track outdoor workouts on its own. Huawei also highlights upgraded health tracking features. The Band 11 Pro adds pulse wave arrhythmia analysis and updated sleep tracking tools.

Also, read

You still get the usual Huawei wearable features like continuous heart rate monitoring, SpO₂ tracking, sleep tracking, stress monitoring, and emotional wellbeing tools. The band uses a 9-axis IMU sensor system along with an optical heart rate sensor and an ambient light sensor. It connects to the Huawei Health app and supports Android 9 and above, and iOS 13 and above. Battery life is also unchanged from the regular Band 11. Huawei claims up to 14 days on light use, around 8 days on typical usage, and roughly 3 days with Always On Display enabled.

In Nepal, the official price of the Huawei Band 11 Pro is NPR 14,990. You can buy this fitness band in Nepal from Huawei-authorized stores across the country.
